page: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, supported by 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz. This configuration yields a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, which is typical for compute-focused workloads. The instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. It does not include local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results demonstrate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression tests all placing in the top tier. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for both cached and uncached operations, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the lower tier. For specialized tasks, the instance delivers top-tier performance in single-threaded text processing and text generation for small language models, while maintaining average throughput for static web serving and Redis operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8a.xlarge offers high-density compute resources with dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for compute-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. The lack of bundled local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is well-suited for single-threaded applications, software compilation, text processing, lightweight web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores, 4 vCPUs, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, establishing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network speed of 2 Gbps and contains no local storage or GPU hardware. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and text processing, while multi-core operations and memory bandwidth remain average. The instance represents a cost-efficient choice for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is highly suited for single-threaded applications, software compilation, text processing, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor at 4.5 GHz, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding single-threaded and text-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz.
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Features a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps via the Nitro hypervisor.
No local storage or GPU accelerators are included in this configuration.
Optimized for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and lightweight inference.
